items.mistake: Training with max_depth=None on small datasets
items.fix: Set max_depth (e.g., 3–8), min_samples_leaf ≥ 5–20, and tune with cross-validation. Use cost-complexity pruning if available.
items.why: Unrestricted trees create one-sample leaves that memorize noise — perfect train accuracy, poor generalization.
items.example: 200 rows, 30 features, max_depth=None → 100% train / 62% val. max_depth=4, min_samples_leaf=10 → 88% train / 71% val.

items.mistake: Feeding raw string categoricals directly to DecisionTreeClassifier
items.fix: One-hot encode, ordinal-encode with domain order, or target-encode before fit. Ensure all columns are numeric.
items.why: scikit-learn CART expects numeric arrays — raw strings cause errors or require separate preprocessing pipelines.
items.example: Wrong: X['city'] = ['NYC', 'LA', ...] passed to fit. Right: pd.get_dummies or OneHotEncoder in a Pipeline.

items.mistake: Assuming Gini and entropy always pick different trees
items.fix: Treat criterion as a minor hyperparameter; tune depth and leaf constraints first. Compare both only if validation differs materially.
items.why: Gini and entropy usually rank candidate splits similarly; large performance gaps are uncommon on standard benchmarks.
items.example: Iris with max_depth=3: gini vs entropy test accuracy often identical or within 1–2%.

items.mistake: Using training accuracy to judge a deep tree
items.fix: Report cross-validated or hold-out metrics; plot train vs validation error vs max_depth to find the sweet spot.
items.why: Deep trees monotonically fit training data better — training accuracy is misleading for model selection.
items.example: depth=20: train acc 1.0, val acc 0.65. depth=5: train acc 0.92, val acc 0.84 — prefer the latter.

items.fix: Skip scaling for pure tree models — splits depend on order and thresholds, not absolute scale.
items.why: StandardScaler changes numeric values but not split rankings on individual features; adds pipeline complexity with no benefit for axis-aligned trees.
items.example: Income in dollars vs thousands: same split structure after monotonic rescaling per feature.
